title: BLE/Zigbee Gateway Health
spl:
index=iot sourcetype IN ("zigbee:gateway","ble:gateway")
| where coordinator_status!="OK" OR offline_devices > 5 OR mqtt_connected=0
| table _time, gateway_id, coordinator_status, offline_devices, mqtt_connected
description: Mesh coordinators and gateways aggregate low-power sensors; their health affects entire buildings.
value: Mesh coordinators and gateways aggregate low-power sensors; their health affects entire buildings.
implementation: Poll hub API for mesh depth, neighbor loss, and MQTT uplink. Alert on coordinator down or offline child count spike.
visualization: Status grid (gateway × health), Line chart (offline device count), Table (gateways degraded).
knownFalsePositives: Topic stalls from broker maintenance, client disconnects, or upstream sensor power cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Zigbee2MQTT, Home Assistant, vendor hub.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="zigbee:gateway"` OR `sourcetype="ble:gateway"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll hub API for mesh depth, neighbor loss, and MQTT uplink. Alert on coordinator down or offline child count spike.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iot sourcetype IN ("zigbee:gateway","ble:gateway")
| where coordinator_status!="OK" OR offline_devices > 5 OR mqtt_connected=0
| table _time, gateway_id, coordinator_status, offline_devices, mqtt_connected
```

Understanding this SPL

**BLE/Zigbee Gateway Health** — Mesh coordinators and gateways aggregate low-power sensors; their health affects entire buildings.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="zigbee:gateway"` OR `sourcetype="ble:gateway"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Zigbee2MQTT, Home Assistant, vendor hub. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: iot.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=iot. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where coordinator_status!="OK" OR offline_devices > 5 OR mqtt_connected=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **BLE/Zigbee Gateway Health**): table _time, gateway_id, coordinator_status, offline_devices, mqtt_connected


Step 3 — Validate
Compare device counts, last-seen times, and health fields to the IoT platform console (for example AWS IoT, Azure IoT Hub, or Google Cloud IoT) for the same period. Verify API polling intervals and that offline rules match your operational definition.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (gateway × health), Line chart (offline device count), Table (gateways degraded).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your connected devices and the messages they send. We help you know if something is off with this area: ble and zigbee gateway before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
